mechanics.text:
ThE CLANS
between Clans or t0 discuss matters affecting the Clans whole Its members have far-ranging authority when dealing with Clan-wide matters but their power strictly limited matlers concerning single Clan The Grand Council traditionally meets in Grand Kurultai, face-to face gathering the Hall ot Khans on the Clan capital of Strana Mechty: also meet in virtual kurultai" via HPG; In times of crisis, the Grand Council can elect an ilKhan war leader with Clan-wide authority: Throughout Clan history, only ten such individuals have been chosen; CLAN POLItiCS Two opposing factions dominate Clan politics The first; known as Wardens_ believes tne Clans should serve as protec tors of the Inner Sphere The second_ called Crusaders advo cates the military conquest of the Inner Sphere and the re formation of the Star League with the Clans at its head; Both factions use the writings and speeches Aleksandr and Nicholas Kerensky t0 justify their positions but demands by the civilian castes t0 return the ~lost paradise" tne  Inner Sphere eventually ed t0 the Crusaders rise Currently. with the destruction of Clan Smoke Jaguar and the Crusaders loss t0 the Inner Sphere the Great Refusal Strana Mechty; the Wardens dominate Clan politics. Though they favor solationism; the Wardens crave martial victories as much as any Clansman: Despite being unwilling participants in the invasion of the Inner Sphere they considered it matter of nonor do their best. That their success capturing territory reduced Crusader influ- ence was added bonus, lesser   division within the Clans aiso exists between those chosen to invade the Inner Sphere and those left behind Animosity flares frequently between these two groups, souring relations both Warden and Crusader camps and leading several cross-faction alliances ECONOMY The Clans have developed strong economy support their military infrastructure, The merchant caste oversees ali aspects of industry, predominantly manufacturing; mining and farming: Monolithic trading houses control the distribution of goods within each Clan and oversee transactions with  otner Clans ad outsiders_ Though the Clans possess such elements of high finance as tutures speculation and the like_ the concept of making money for its own sake foreign to them. By and large_ the Clans have no use tor casn Instead_ they use wOrk credit, essentially barter system for acquiring goods and services from the Clans central authority- Any work credit not used reverts tO the controe 0t the Clan that earned it; addition_ goods remain the property of the Clan ad may be redistributed at will: The Clans' single currency; called kerensky symbol: KEI, used almost exclusively by the merchant caste electronic form t0 smooth mercantile exchanges ad allow financial insti- tutions operate_ Hard cash exists for  limited use -small coins 10 ad 20 kerensky denominations and rectan- gular blocks in 100. 1,000. 100,000 and OOO,00O denomi-
nations The coins and blocks are pressed in Eold and contain chips and chemical compounds to prevent forgery: By the best available estimate_ the average exchange rate is Keren- sky C-bills The primary distinction between Clan and Inner Sphere eco nomic systems IS the motivating force behind them. The Clans trade for what they need; the Inner Sphere gives people what they want. Efficiency is centra Clan economics, and recycling IS an essential part of daily Ilife. Clan merchants deal mainly essentials rather than luxuries_ for which the average Clansman sees little use and 50 does not value However; trickle of Iux- ury goods from the Inner Sphere has recently begun arriving in Clan space, and some predict that the Inner Sphere' consumer culture will bring down the Clans from within.
TECHNOLOGY The advanced weaponry used by the Clans has spawned the belief that all Clan technology vastly superior t0 the Inner Sphere Clan technology is undoubtedly more advanced; how- ever, tne gap not as wide as it initially appeared_ The Clans have focused most of their research on military technologies weapons genetics computing and materials chemistry: They nave  made Tew advances in civilian technologies , where Clans lag far behind the Inner Sphere. The Clans' fixation on the Star League has also stagnated ther technological develop ment. Much of the equipment they use differs little from that of the Star League the Iate twenty eighth century: The one exception medical technology; where spin-offs from the eugenics program have given the Clans phenomenal advantage Using highly advanced life support and regeneration techniques_ the Clans can regrow ay part of the body save the brain; Their   genetic moditication techniques are similarly sophisticated_ The eugenics program uses these extensively; along with artificial gestation systems known as iron wombs Though such systems are far from new; the need for rapid pOp ulation increase prompted Nicholas Kerensky and his scientific teams t0 abandon the ethica restrictions the use such technologies that had existed since the end of the twentieth century. The Clans have since accepted artificial gestation s0 completely that many Clansmen regard natural birth as gusting and unclean
CLAN SOCIETY AND CULTURE Most works published on the Clans have focused almost entirely their military, their Inner Sphere holdings their technology:  Little has been written about Clan society wnole and many tne existing works are biased_ better understanding of Clan civilian culture might enable the people of the Inner Sphere coexist peacefully with the descendants of Aleksandr Kerensky:
